text: Markets work best when regulation is light and entrepreneurs can experiment freely.
Lower corporate taxes attract investment; that lifts wages over time.
Free trade expands consumer choice; tariffs are a tax on families.
School choice empowers parents; competition improves public schools too.
Energy abundance requires permitting reform and predictable rules.
Deficit spending should be reserved for real emergencies, not routine programs.
Immigration policy should prioritize skills and assimilation.
Tech innovation outpaces regulators; sandbox rules beat blanket bans.
Housing costs fall when we build more homes and cut red tape.
Federal agencies should face stricter cost-benefit tests.
Policing works best with community cooperation and clear standards.
NATO burden-sharing matters; allies should meet defense spending goals.
Work requirements for some benefits restore dignity and fiscal sanity.
Carbon pricing beats command-and-control if revenues are returned.
Antitrust should target consumer harm, not company size alone.
Judges should interpret law as written, not legislate from the bench.
Federalism lets states compete; one-size policy fails diverse communities.
Public unions should not dominate school boards or pension policy.
Entitlement reform is math, not ideology; younger workers need options.
Border security and legal immigration pathways are both necessary.
Crypto innovation needs clarity, not whack-a-mole enforcement.
Rural broadband improves telehealth and small business formation.
Tort reform reduces defensive medicine and lowers insurance costs.
Space policy should prioritize launch costs and commercial partners.
Labor law should protect gig workers without destroying flexible work.
Veterans deserve timely healthcare and skills training for civilian jobs.
Farm subsidies distort markets; risk management should be targeted.
Nuclear is a reliable zero-carbon bridge while storage scales.
Criminal justice reform should focus on nonviolent offenses and recidivism.
Patent thickets slow biotech; faster review helps patients.
Infrastructure dollars should fix maintenance before shiny new projects.
Higher education accreditation should reward outcomes, not inputs.
Zoning reform is bipartisan: let people build duplexes near transit.
Defense innovation requires faster procurement and smaller programs.
Welfare cliffs trap families; phaseouts should be smoother.
AI regulation should be sector-specific and risk-based.
Trade deals should include digital rules that protect speech and IP.
Police qualified immunity needs careful balance with accountability.
Medicaid block grants can improve incentives if guardrails exist.
We should expand apprenticeships as a college alternative.
Today I am focused on climate justice, public ownership of energy, and universal care.
Markets alone cannot deliver equity; we need industrial planning and worker boards.
Private healthcare profits are incompatible with moral outcomes; single-payer now.
Borders are arbitrary lines; migration is a human right, not a policy debate.
Police and prisons cannot be reformed; abolition is the only ethical path.
Tech billionaires captured regulators; we should nationalize platforms.
